summary.intent: Fix a bug in the MCP SSE transport's is_alive() health check and get it reviewed and merged via PR #733.
summary.outcome: The one-line fix was pushed, PR #733 was updated, Greptile review completed, stale comments were dismissed, valid comments were addressed in mcp.spec.js, and the fixes were committed and pushed.

repo: Greptile is used as an automated code review tool on PRs in the moltis-org/moltis repo.
MCP tests live under crates/mcp and there are 93 of them; E2E specs live under crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/.
The repo uses a worktree-based workflow at ~/.superset/worktrees/moltis/<branch-name>.

code.finding: is_alive() was incorrectly returning false for non-2xx/non-401 HTTP responses; fix makes it return true for any HTTP response.
E2E assertions and test names needed updating to match the corrected is_alive() behavior; Greptile flagged these as valid issues after the fix commit.

workflow: Greptile review comments can become stale immediately after a fix commit — always diff current code against each comment before acting.
Greptile output can contain control characters that break naive jq parsing; simpler/more defensive jq queries are needed.
The Greptile review loop polls for check completion before fetching inline comments and resolving threads.

friction: Greptile output contained control characters that caused jq parsing to fail, requiring a fallback to a simpler query.
Two of four Greptile comments were already stale by the time the review ran, requiring manual triage against current code.
open_items: Next Greptile review iteration (iteration 2/5) was triggered at the end of the transcript but results are not yet available.
